raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 1108 will require independent medical examinations into the deaths of individuals who die in-custody.",
    "California is one of only three States that allow sheriffs to simultaneously serve as coroners.",
    "Elected sheriffs in 48 counties across California are tasked with investigating all suspicious and unattended deaths, including those that occur in their custody.",
    "The same officials responsible for keeping incarcerated individuals safe also conduct death investigations and medical examinations after a death in jail.",
    "A 2024 Santa Barbara County Grand Jury report identified conflicts of interest arising from sheriffs serving as coroners.",
    "In-custody deaths demand independent oversight to maintain public trust and ensure transparency.",
    "AB 1108 aims to eliminate conflicts of interest by requiring impartial medical examinations of in-custody deaths.",
    "The legislation reflects a growing consensus that independent review is essential to upholding justice and public confidence."
  ]
}
```
